judgment.summary: Dominant, highly profitable, and rapidly growing semiconductor manufacturer trading at a reasonable forward multiple.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimate by 4.7%; next earnings date is 2026-07-16.
positives: Revenue growth 35.1% YoY and Earnings growth 58.4% YoY demonstrate robust top and bottom-line expansion.
Profit margin 46.5% and Operating margin 58.1% are exceptionally high, indicating strong pricing power and operational efficiency.
Forward P/E of 20.65 is attractive given the high growth rates and strong profitability.
Free cash flow of $719.16B indicates significant cash generation capabilities.
Analyst consensus is 'strong buy' with a mean target price of $467.84, suggesting considerable upside potential.
negatives: Price/Book ratio of 89.19 is very high, suggesting significant market premium.
Beta of 1.26 indicates higher volatility than the broader market.
Total debt of $1.09T is substantial, though covered by significant cash and cash flow.

news.paragraph: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) is garnering positive attention, with Jim Cramer expressing strong approval and one analysis highlighting its significant upside potential within the semiconductor sector. While broader market futures show a dip, TSM's individual outlook appears robust. The upcoming earnings report on July 16th will be a key event to watch.

headlines.title: Jim Cramer on Taiwan Semi: “The More I Look at It, the More I Like It”
